LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF TROY KING'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS). DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL, MR. MARCHI, TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO TROY KING'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION, DEFENDANT BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRERS TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. THE CCP 340 ONE-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS APPLIES TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LIBEL PER SE AND THE ONE PLEADED COMMUNICATION WITHIN THE YEAR CANNOT BE CONSTRUED AS LIBELOUS. ALSO, PUBLICATION OF THAT ONE COMMUNICATION TO A THIRD PERSON IS NOT ALLEGED. THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FAILS TO PLEAD FACTS OF EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED BY NOVEMBER 2, 2018. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Troy King, 415-684-0935 Plaintiff in Pro Per.
Robert Marchi, Esq. 415-464-8888 x236 of Bradley Curley Asiano Barrabee Abel & Kowalski P.C. for Defendant BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF SAN FRANCISCO through the Court's telephone.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares the basis for it's tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues statute of limitation and tolling. Plaintiff argues. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO TROY KING'S 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendant Boys & Girls Clubs of San Francisco's demurrer to Plaintiff Troy King's second amended complaint is sustained as to the sole cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress without leave to amend. Mr. King's intentional infliction of emotional distress claim is time-barred. Claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress have a two-year statute of limitations. (Code Civ. Proc. 335.1; Wassmann v. South Orange Cty. Comm. College Dist. (2018) 24 Cal.App.5th 825, 852-53.) "A cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress accrues, and the statute of limitations begins to run, once the plaintiff suffers severe emotional distress as a result of outrageous conduct on the part of the defendant." (Wassmann, 24 Cal.App.5th at 853.) Liberally construing the second amended complaint, Mr. King claims to have suffered emotional distress as a result of learning in 2012 that Club employee Nate Ford had full knowledge that a coworker, rather than Mr. King, had been guilty of sexual involvement with a minor of which Mr. King had been accused in 2001, and that Mr. Ford failed to report the information to absolve Mr. King from blame and restore his reputation. Mr. King did not file his original complaint in this action until May 21, 2018, more than two years after 2012, so his cause of action is barred. It is appropriate to sustain a demurrer without leave to amend where the claim is time-barred. (Yee v. Mobile Home Park Rental Review Bd. (1998) 62 Cal.App.4th 1409, 1429.)

Mr. King's citation to Juarez v. Boy Scouts of America, Inc. (2000) 81 Cal.App.4th 377 is inapposite as that case involves a heightened duty of care of businesses to children, not to an employee or former employee such as Mr. King. Moreover, the minor's 2017 text messages carry no weight here. Even if they were referenced in the second amended complaint-and they are not-these messages cannot form the basis for Defendant's liability as she is alleged to be a former client of Defendant, not its employee or agent. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Defendant's counsel Mr. Marchi to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Peter F. Finn of Bradley Curley, Tel. No. 1-415-464-8888 ext. 275 appearing
for the Defendant;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BOYS & GIRLS CLUBS OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant's demurrers to the first amended complaint are SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. The CCP 340 one-year statute of limitations applies to the cause of action for libel per se and the one pleaded communication within the year cannot be construed as libelous. Also, publication of that one communication to a third person is not alleged. The cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress fails to plead facts of extreme and outrageous conduct. Amended Complaint to be filed by November 2, 2018. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600
